title: FULL Charlie Kirk Debate VS Wyoming College Students
body: Guy from WyoTech... you're doing it right. I worked in in an orchard when I was younger and had my Private Pilot Certificate my junior year of high school. When I graduated high school I got a paid maintenance apprenticeship and put that money into continued flying lessons. Went into Army National Guard Aviation at 22. I'm 37, no student loan debt, am a Corporate Pilot, Certified Flight Instructor, due to my time as an apprentice, am a licensed Airframe and Powerplant Mechanic, & Army Aviator. Charlie's right on that.
author: @BH-vy5vw

title: FULL Charlie Kirk Debate VS Wyoming College Students
body:
I am an adjunct professor, and I could not agree more that we push 4-year education way too hard. It’s not only diluted the value of a degree (it’s become essentially a HS degree for some fields), but we have capped earning potential for some individuals. I promote trade schools often. While I find value in college and earning an education, I do not see the value in flooding enrollment and making it mandatory for respect.

My brother is a lineman. He went to trade school for 15-weeks. He consistently makes over $320k/year. His highest earning year was $467k. I have multiple degrees. Have a good job. Do just fine. But I will never make that type of money because my earning potential is capped in what I studied and the path I chose. Unless my wealth comes from an independent venture, I will never come close to that money as an employee.

Let’s promote trade schools. Let’s promote alternatives to a 4-year degree. Obviously, specialized fields are incredibly important and college is necessary and needed. Charlie is right that we push out way too many social science, communication, and general business degrees. But college shouldn’t be the expectation for all. Your value shouldn’t be held in whether you have a degree/went to college or not. At the end of the day, it is a business. And college operates under a business model now more than ever. And it should be viewed as such so individuals can make a better informed decision on what they want to do post-HS.
author: @Ozymandias2424

title: University of Wyoming Campus Tour
body: I was there in 2008 as an exchange student for 1 semester only. Lots of great memories with the McIntyre Hall, Washakie Dining Center and my walk to classes through Prexy's Pasture everyday. I keep telling myself that one day I will go back there to relive all those wonderful memories.

title: University of Wyoming Review (Advice for Incoming Students) | Laramie Review | 2020 | Lizzy Hinshaw
body:
I'm going to Wyoming for a summer research placement in July, I'm flying in all the way from Wales so I have no idea what studying in the US is going to be like! One main question I have is if there are people around in July? I dont know how your guys' term dates work but I really hope I'm not going to turn up to a completely dead campus with no one to befriend :(

Also how is it getting around without a car? I have a license but I'm under 21 so I don't think I'll be able to rent a car but I'd still want to go to places slightly further (Walmart for example) Thanks
